case_metadata.title: Living-Off-the-Land (LOTL) PowerShell Attack
case_metadata.scenario_class: LOTL attack using signed Windows binaries to evade AV
case_metadata.ground_truth_provenance: Authored alongside the bundled sample evidence; the process tree, event log entries, and persistence artefacts are deterministic in the fixture.

ground_truth_findings.claim: powershell.exe spawning cmd.exe and wscript.exe — LOTL parent-child pattern
ground_truth_findings.rationale: PowerShell PID 4812 spawned cmd.exe 4820 and wscript.exe 4824 — unusual for benign workflows.

ground_truth_findings.claim: LSASS access with GrantedAccess=0x1FFFFF (credential dumping signature)
ground_truth_findings.rationale: Sysmon EventID 10 with full-access mask = mimikatz-class signature.
EventID 4104 PowerShell script block contains classic in-memory loader.

ground_truth_findings.claim: Registry Run key 'SecurityUpdate' with hidden PowerShell DownloadString
ground_truth_findings.rationale: Run key contains 'powershell -w hidden -c IEX(DownloadString(...))' command.
Service binary path in user Temp folder — diagnostic of attacker persistence.

ground_truth_findings.claim: PowerShell script block + LSASS access correlated in 2-minute window
ground_truth_findings.rationale: DuckDB scale engine cross-source correlation links script execution to credential dumping by same actor.
Same persistence artefact as case-01 — demonstrates cross-case attribution.
